vocab.meaning: khám phá một mình/không có người đi kèm
vocab.example: Back then, the neighborhood was safe enough for us to engage in unaccompanied exploration every afternoon.

vocab.meaning: những con phố dân cư rợp bóng cây
vocab.example: I have vivid memories of strolling through the leafy residential streets near my primary school.

vocab.meaning: niềm đam mê khám phá/đi đây đi đó
vocab.example: Even as a child, walking ignited a sense of wanderlust in me, making me curious about what lay beyond my street.

vocab.meaning: đi ngang qua/băng qua khu xóm
vocab.example: My friends and I would traverse the neighborhood for hours, discovering hidden shortcuts between houses.

vocab.meaning: lối sống ít vận động/thụ động
vocab.example: Unlike kids today who are often glued to screens, I didn't have a sedentary lifestyle because I was always out walking.
I used to walk to the local park just to commune with nature and escape the noise of my large family.
My siblings and I would often scamper along the riverbank, looking for flat stones to skip across the water.

vocab.meaning: một người rất thích đi bộ
vocab.example: My grandfather was an avid walker, and he would frequently take me on his long morning treks.

vocab.meaning: những con hẻm nhỏ cổ kính/độc đáo
vocab.example: We loved wandering through the quaint back alleys of our town, which felt like a labyrinth to our young minds.

vocab.meaning: sự nuôi dưỡng gắn liền với các hoạt động ngoài trời
vocab.example: Having an outdoor upbringing meant that walking was our primary form of entertainment and discovery.
